name: enhanced-context-aware-job-matching
hypothesis: Implementing advanced semantic job matching with company culture analysis, predictive success scoring, and contextual relevance weighting will improve the practical utility of job matches by 30% while maintaining current discrimination levels.

key_findings.high_confidence_achievements: 88.16% average confidence with 100% high confidence matches
key_findings.semantic_advancements: Advanced understanding of job intent and company culture
key_findings.utility_tradeoffs: Lower average match score (61.84) due to stricter evaluation criteria

lessons_learned.enhanced_semantic_matching: Provides deeper understanding but may be more conservative in scoring
lessons_learned.culture_analysis_importance: Company culture matching significantly impacts prediction accuracy
lessons_learned.predictive_confidence_vs_score: High confidence doesn't always translate to high match scores
lessons_learned.need_for_balanced_approach: Stricter criteria improves prediction reliability but may reduce overall match rates
